title: Winey Snappy Venezuelan Salmon Empanadas
description: Crispy empanadas filled with a flavorful salmon and onion mixture, served with a side of tangy wine sauce. A high-protein, kid-friendly recipe perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillet
ingredients.notes: Fresh and skinless for best results

ingredients.name: Wine
ingredients.notes: Dry white wine, such as Chardonnay or Sauvignon Blanc

instructions: Cut the salmon fillet into small pieces and set aside.
Heat the corn oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion to the skillet and cook until caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the salmon to the skillet and cook until cooked through, about 3-4 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg and water.
Place the dough on a floured surface and roll out to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the salmon mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the egg wash and fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle.
Press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the melted butter.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 15-20 minutes or until golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, prepare the wine sauce by reducing the wine over low heat until almost syrupy.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and let cool for a few minutes.
Serve the empanadas with the wine sauce and enjoy!
